---
title: "Career Readiness Assessment"
author: "Grad Guide"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
---

# Career Readiness Assessment

## Core Checklist

- [ ] Polished resume
- [ ] Cover letter template
- [ ] Updated LinkedIn
- [ ] Portfolio/projects
- [ ] Interview practice
- [ ] Target roles identified
- [ ] List of companies
- [ ] Networking actively
- [ ] Applied to jobs

---

## Readiness Summary

**If 1–3 items completed (Foundation stage)**  
Focus on building your basic career materials, identifying goals, and starting to network.

**If 4–6 items completed (Preparation stage)**  
Refine materials, practice interview skills, and start actively networking.

**If 7–9 items completed (Active application stage)**  
You are ready to consistently apply, network, and prepare for interviews.

---

## Recommended Next Steps

**Foundation stage (1–3 items completed)**

- Build and refine your resume  
- Create a basic cover letter template  
- Set up or update LinkedIn profile  
- Start identifying target roles  

**Preparation stage (4–6 items completed)**

- Polish portfolio or project examples  
- Practice common interview questions  
- Update and tailor cover letter for roles  
- Expand networking outreach  

**Active application stage (7–9 items completed)**

- Apply to at least 5 jobs per week  
- Follow-up with contacts and recruiters  
- Prepare for interviews with mock sessions  
- Maintain and update portfolio/projects as needed  

---

## Weekly Action Plan

**Foundation stage (1–3 items completed)**

- Work on 1–2 key career materials per week  
- Reach out to 1–2 professionals for networking  
- Start listing companies and roles of interest  

**Preparation stage (4–6 items completed)**

- Apply to 3–5 roles per week  
- Reach out to 2–3 new contacts  
- Practice interviews twice per week  

**Active application stage (7–9 items completed)**

- Apply to 5–10 jobs per week  
- Network with 3–5 new professionals  
- Continue refining interview skills  
- Track applications and follow-ups  

---

## Final Thought

Career readiness comes from consistent action. Focus on completing your items for your current stage, then advance to the next.
